title: [EPIC]: Release 2 Codebase Refactor — Establish a clean, typed, tested structural baseline
label: epic
summary: Refactor the existing MVP codebase into a disciplined Release 2 baseline that improves type safety, removes the specifically identified duplication, reduces oversized hotspots, standardises error handling, and adds meaningful automated tests around refactored core paths. R2 is structural only and must preserve existing behaviour.
problem_hl: The current codebase was built incrementally by humans and chatbots during MVP development, resulting in duplicated logic, inconsistent patterns, weak typing (widespread use of 'any'), no automated tests, missing linting/formatting, oversized monolithic components, scattered hardcoded values, and debug logging left in production paths. This makes the code fragile, hard to reason about, and expensive to extend — an unreliable foundation for future releases.
goals: Eliminate the specifically identified high-value DRY violations: extract shared hooks for stages/sub-stages, unify the API fetch layer, centralise shared constants, and remove repeated route id parsing

Improve type safety across the application and server by removing 'any' from refactored/core code paths and defining explicit contracts for storage, API request/response shapes, component props, and domain entities
Decompose the named oversized hotspots (Admin, Dashboard, KanbanBoard, EditTaskDialog) into clearer single-responsibility modules without changing behaviour
Introduce meaningful automated test coverage for extracted shared logic, storage contracts, critical route paths, and refactored UI hotspots
Establish and enforce code quality tooling: ESLint with strict TypeScript rules, Prettier for formatting, Husky pre-commit hooks, and CI-ready npm scripts (lint, format, test, check)
Standardise error handling with consistent server-side error responses and a client-side React ErrorBoundary
Replace production debug logging with a configurable logger gated by environment
Produce a cleaner R2 baseline that is safer to extend in later feature work
scope: Phase 1: Tooling & guardrails — Add ESLint (strict TS), Prettier, lint-staged + Husky pre-commit, npm scripts (lint, format, test, check). Fix linter issues in application code touched by R2. Configure Vitest with reporting.

Phase 2: Type safety & contracts — Remove 'any' from core/refactored code paths. Define and export interfaces/contracts for Stage, SubStage, Task, InsertTask, API responses, storage methods, and component props. Verify and harden strict TypeScript settings already in place.

Phase 3: DRY extraction & shared modules — Create useStages and useSubStages hooks to replace duplicated inline queries. Unify fetch calls behind a single typed apiRequest wrapper. Extract shared constants used across the app. Create a parseIdParam helper for repeated parseInt/isNaN handling in server routes.
Phase 4: Server structure cleanup — Move seed data out of route registration into a dedicated seed/bootstrap module or equivalent isolated location. Keep behaviour unchanged.

Phase 5: Component decomposition — Split Admin.tsx, Dashboard.tsx, KanbanBoard.tsx, and EditTaskDialog.tsx into clearer focused modules. Use 200 LOC as a preferred target for resulting modules, but do not force artificial splits where readability would worsen.

Phase 6: Error handling & logging — Implement Express error-handling middleware with typed/custom error handling and consistent JSON responses. Add a React ErrorBoundary at the app root. Replace console.log/console.error with a lightweight logger gated by NODE_ENV. Fix the rethrow-after-response bug in server/index.ts. Stop request logging from leaking full response bodies.

Phase 7: Tests — Add Vitest tests for extracted hooks, utility/shared modules, storage contract behaviour, and critical API route paths. Add focused component tests for refactored UI hotspots where logic was moved or isolated.

Phase 8: Cleanup & closeout — Remove unused dependencies only where confirmed unused. Remove or gate Replit-specific plugins behind environment checks. Update key architecture/component docs to reflect the new structure.
out_of_scope: New user-facing features — R2 is purely structural; no new UI capabilities, screens, or workflows
Database schema changes — the existing Drizzle schema and migrations remain unchanged
Migration to a different framework, ORM, or state management library
Performance optimisation (bundle splitting, lazy loading, caching) — deferred to a dedicated performance epic
CI/CD pipeline setup — tooling is CI-ready but pipeline configuration is a separate concern
Visual/design refresh — styling and theming remain as-is
Broad codebase-wide cleanup not directly tied to the debt explicitly identified in this epic
Release administration tasks such as version tagging and changelog generation
acceptance: npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
ESLint passes with zero errors for the application codebase in scope for R2
All stage/sub-stage data fetching uses shared hooks (useStages, useSubStages) with no inline duplication for those resources outside the hooks
All API calls in refactored paths route through a single typed apiRequest wrapper
Repeated route id parsing is replaced by a shared helper in refactored server routes
The known server error-handler crash caused by rethrowing after sending a response is fixed
Production debug logging is removed or routed through the logger module
Server error responses follow a consistent JSON shape for handled 4xx/5xx responses
A React ErrorBoundary catches and renders fallback UI for unhandled client errors
The named oversized hotspots are decomposed into clearer modules with preserved behaviour
Automated tests exist for extracted shared logic, storage contract behaviour, and critical route paths touched by the refactor
No database schema changes, no feature changes, and no visual redesign are introduced
